117451.explanation: Quality of candidates comes first; if there is a comparable level, then it is fine to consider a balanced distribution between men and women, as that certainly can add something.

117455.explanation: Yes, but only in good consultation with residents due to possible nuisance.

117458.explanation: We do not decide on this; it is an obligation from the national government.

117463.explanation: Yes, we want to periodically collect bulky waste in the neighborhoods. We now see it regularly placed next to trash bins, which does not make Krimpen look nicer.

117465.explanation: We support an inclusive municipality, but the responsibility of the municipality is limited to municipal buildings. Entrepreneurs should take responsibility themselves.

117466.explanation: Sports is more than movement; it connects people and brings them out of isolation.

117471.explanation: It is good to preserve our culture.

117472.explanation: We want more community officers (BOAs) and also that they are more visible. Moreover, we want to add a youth community officer specifically trained to engage young people.

117474.explanation: If the necessity is present. If there are sufficient alternatives, it is not necessary.

117475.explanation: Due to a shortage at the police, community officers often arrive first on the scene. It is desirable that BOAs at least have something to defend themselves if the need arises.

117479.explanation: Where possible, but in our opinion this could also be done without subsidies. Above all, we agree that offices can be converted into housing.

117480.explanation: The principle is that everyone contributes to society.

117482.explanation: May remain the same, often currently at 130% of the social minimum.

117484.explanation: We also see spots for electric cars that are not used, resulting in fewer spaces for non-electric cars. So first, a good inventory and a well-considered choice per location is needed.

117487.explanation: With the staff shortage, this does not seem feasible to us.

117489.explanation: Krimpen is already an expensive municipality, so wherever possible it can be reduced.

117492.explanation: In the Stormpolder it is good to stimulate business activity, but on other locations not, so it depends on the location.

117494.explanation: We must comply with national obligations, but no more than that.

117496.explanation: But even more preferably, we want housing.

117500.explanation: If a demand comes from an entrepreneur, we will assess it objectively, taking into account the opinions of residents and neighbors.

117502.explanation: We will have to because Krimpen is fully built up and there is a shortage of housing. We must pay enough attention to maintaining the right balance.

117503.explanation: That would be good for our youth.

117504.explanation: Where there is a need, we will certainly encourage it. This fits with our district-related approach.

117505.explanation: No more and no less. The current balance is fine.

117506.explanation: When pleasant terraces replace it, that is fine; if not, we keep it as it is now.

117509.explanation: Events connect people; therefore, we want to involve youth, sports clubs, and entrepreneurs so that more events are organized. To help them, we also want to relax regulations on organizing events and provide support.

117451.explanation: In the past period, two Leefbaar aldermen have served on the council: one woman and one man. Of course, appointments should be based on competence, but when equally qualified, a mix of women and men is preferred.

117455.explanation: Leefbaar is a strong advocate for liveliness and conviviality, but we must agree on rules together. Without fixed opening hours, the chance of nuisance increases and enforcement becomes very difficult.

117458.explanation: Leefbaar is not in favor of a location for large-scale asylum reception (such as a reception center) on land. The asylum boat is the limit. We remain vigilant on housing allocation, livability, and enforcement.

117463.explanation: Illegal dumping must be prevented as much as possible and tackled firmly. To anticipate this problem and make it more convenient for our seniors and apartment residents, we want to make free bulky waste collection possible. However, this is very expensive, so we see a pilot of six times per year.

117465.explanation: Accessibility is the entrepreneur’s own responsibility, who will notice a lack of it through, for example, fewer customers. The municipality can promote or encourage inclusivity but should not pay for it. The municipality does contribute to the accessibility of its own buildings and municipal grounds.

117466.explanation: Sports are incredibly valuable and contribute not only to physical development but also to mental and social development, for example. Membership of sports clubs should remain accessible to those with fewer means, because all Krimpen residents should be able to participate.

117471.explanation: There is no need to cut budgets in Krimpen at the moment, so this is not relevant.

117472.explanation: For Leefbaar, safety is high on the agenda, so we welcome extra enforcement officers. We want to shape this additional safety effort by introducing district managers as contact points for neighborhood issues and appointing a 'green enforcement officer' for nuisance around waste.

117474.explanation: Leefbaar stands for safety and livability. A shelter for domestic violence victims offers refuge to people facing serious domestic violence or threats. Such a safe and anonymous refuge must also exist in Krimpen.

117475.explanation: Enforcement efforts are crucial to keep Krimpen safe and livable, but the safety of those carrying them out must also be guaranteed. The worrying increase in knife possession also calls for a way for our enforcement officers to defend themselves in emergency situations.

117479.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, it is crucial to seize coupling opportunities. When office buildings remain empty for a long time, they should be handled smartly and creatively by converting them into housing. Krimpen residents deserve a Krimpen home.

117480.explanation: Preferably people can move on to paid jobs, but those for whom this does not succeed can also be valuable. Everyone can contribute in their own way to society. This also brings purpose and meaning to their day.

117482.explanation: In recent years, Leefbaar has consistently chosen a limit of 130% of the minimum income for minimum regulations, such as free public transport. The municipality is not a bank that needs to save money but serves Krimpen residents who need this help.

117484.explanation: Leefbaar wants innovative charging points (for example, lampposts with charging options), but this must not come at the expense of existing parking spaces. Due to high parking pressure, we cannot afford to lose parking spaces, so we only look for extra (or new) charging options.

117487.explanation: Leefbaar finds it important that Krimpen residents retain autonomy and may make their own choices. It is not up to the municipality to impose from which care provider residents receive care.

117489.explanation: Leefbaar has ensured that the property tax (ozb) has remained low over recent years. As soon as there is financial room, the property tax must go down.

117492.explanation: Leefbaar prioritizes building homes over expanding the Stormpolder. If business activity can be expanded within the current boundaries of the Stormpolder, this is not a problem.

117494.explanation: First Krimpen. Leefbaar wants priority for Krimpen residents for social rental homes and new build (purchase) projects. We oppose granting priority to refugees with a residence permit, although this is determined nationally and regulated by law.

117496.explanation: This is not relevant in Krimpen, because we have no agricultural land.

117500.explanation: Leefbaar opposes bringing in Rotterdam’s problems. A coffee shop does not fit our village character and we keep them out.

117502.explanation: Leefbaar builds close to facilities and keeps neighborhoods livable. To meet housing demand, we build compact and upwards where needed, but with respect for our village character. No extreme high-rise or too many apartment blocks, so we prevent becoming a suburb of Rotterdam.

117503.explanation: Room rental by slumlords puts livability under pressure. Leefbaar wants to preserve family homes for families and starters. Residential neighborhoods are for living, not to be crammed with separate rooms.

117504.explanation: Leefbaar stands for lively neighborhoods, which definitely include a community center. In Krimpen, these community centers have already been realized.

117505.explanation: Leefbaar is in favor of more parking spaces in the center. The heart of Krimpen must remain accessible for all residents, especially seniors and disabled people. Parking remains free and possible.

117506.explanation: Leefbaar opposes driving motorists out. The Hart van Krimpen project, with which we want to create a Krimpen village center, is an exception area that we do want to make car-free.

117507.explanation: Empty shops in De Crimpenhof are really an eyesore. Leefbaar believes owners must take responsibility. If that does not happen, the municipality must intervene to prevent deterioration of the Crimpenhof.

117509.explanation: Leefbaar has worked hard over the past period to organize great events, many of which have been successful. We will continue to commit to events for all target groups (children, youth, adults, and seniors) to make our Krimpen increasingly more beautiful and lively.

117451.explanation: For the SGP, the qualities of the alderman are more important than an equal balance of men and women.

117455.explanation: If cafés and restaurants set their own opening hours, only one interest is considered: that of the entrepreneur. The SGP wants to take other interests into account in this consideration, including peace on Sundays in Krimpen.

117458.explanation: The SGP supports showing compassion to neighbors in need, including asylum seekers. It is important, however, to pay attention to support and capacity within a neighborhood or district.

117463.explanation: The SGP believes that the polluter should also pay. Due to increased waste processing costs, the burden on residents is rising significantly. Separate collection (bringing residual waste and collecting other streams) and paying per bag of residual waste is most effective for both the wallet and the environment.

117465.explanation: The SGP finds it important that everyone can participate and therefore entrepreneurs should be encouraged to make and keep their buildings accessible for people with disabilities. At the same time, the SGP sees this as a financial responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves, not the municipality.

117466.explanation: The SGP considers it important to encourage physical activity among people with a lower socioeconomic status. Research shows that these people have an increased risk of obesity.

117471.explanation: Krimpen is still building its history and must make conscious choices about which monuments are truly valuable and worth preserving.

117472.explanation: There are 5 special enforcement officers (boa's) active in Krimpen. This currently appears sufficient to address safety issues. If these problems increase, the SGP supports expansion.

117474.explanation: The SGP considers sufficient shelter capacity for domestic violence shelters important and wants to provide generous support if needed in Krimpen. At the same time, the nature of such a shelter (location must remain secret) does not align with a public campaign for this shelter in Krimpen.

117475.explanation: The safety situation in Krimpen is not such that boa's need batons to perform their duties.

117479.explanation: The SGP supports transforming offices into homes but believes it is the responsibility of project developers to create a viable business case. The SGP does see a municipal responsibility to create good conditions for this.

117480.explanation: The SGP favors support focused on recovery. We help residents return as quickly as possible to their own responsibility and independence. Meaningful daytime activities during benefit receipt contribute to this and increase self-esteem. At the same time: those who genuinely cannot work are not required to.

117482.explanation: Thanks in part to the SGP, many local arrangements for low-income individuals have been made available to more people: up to 130% of the social assistance level. We want to maintain these arrangements but not expand them further.

117484.explanation: The SGP finds it important that there are sufficient public charging points. At the same time, the SGP wants to be mindful of residents who cannot (or do not) purchase an electric car and subsequently lose their parking spaces.

117487.explanation: Good care better matches residents who need care when that care aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. In this way, the care provided yields better results.

117489.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is indispensable for the municipality to maintain the high level of amenities in Krimpen. The SGP wants only inflation correction on the ozb but does not want to lower it.

117492.explanation: The SGP supports a flourishing business park in De Stormpolder. Expansion can take place, among other things, after remediation of the former EMK site, where the SGP wants to focus on maritime manufacturing industry along waterways (Hollandsche IJssel, Sliksloot, Nieuwe Maas).

117494.explanation: The SGP wants social rental housing allocations to consider the impact on existing residents. This contributes to a good balance between ‘applicants’ and ‘bearers’ in the neighborhood. A system where a certain category of housing seekers automatically receives priority does not fit here.

117496.explanation: There is hardly any agricultural land in Krimpen. The SGP believes green areas should never be sacrificed for solar panels. Furthermore, there are still plenty of roofs without solar panels.

117500.explanation: A coffee shop does not fit the village character of Krimpen aan den IJssel.

117502.explanation: The village character of Krimpen poorly tolerates high-rise buildings. The 'low high-rise' as realized in Centrum-Zuid is, in the SGP’s view, the standard for other high-rise buildings in Krimpen. Moreover, 'true' high-rise often negatively impacts livability (such as with the Vijverflats).

117503.explanation: Renting out single rooms is not permitted in Krimpen. This helps the municipality in enforcement and prevents nuisance caused by students or labor migrants.

117504.explanation: Krimpen has six districts. The SGP wants every district to have a community center because these 'district rooms' can make an important contribution to residents' self-reliance and thereby to the resilience of neighborhoods.

117505.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces in the center. However, availability for residents of the center is under pressure. The SGP therefore wants to focus on expanding and enforcing the blue zone so that residents of the center (with an exemption) have sufficient parking.

117506.explanation: Krimpen residents must be able to park in the center. The SGP wants to make the Heart of Krimpen car-reduced by blocking the through route over the Raadhuisplein, so that only destination traffic for the center can use this route.

117507.explanation: The SGP favors a renovation of the shopping center where vacant shop space is filled again. By actively investing as a municipality in the surroundings of the Crimpenhof, we encourage the owners to take action. Coercion is a last resort and currently not applicable.

117509.explanation: The current events policy meets the needs of all residents. These can be small-scale at neighborhood level or centrally at the event site. The SGP sees no need to increase the number of events.

117451.explanation: Leaders should be chosen based on quality, experience, and suitability, not gender. A mandatory balance limits freedom of choice and may compromise leadership quality. Diversity is important but should not be forced.

117455.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have as much freedom as possible to run their business. Extended opening hours provide space for entrepreneurship and liveliness, as long as public order, safety, and livability for residents are guaranteed.

117458.explanation: The VVD believes that municipalities must provide humane and safe shelter within legal frameworks. Closing shelter locations on short notice is often not feasible and can lead to unsafe situations for residents and surroundings.

117463.explanation: The VVD believes residents should take responsibility for waste. We want to encourage our residents to sort their waste better and produce less waste.

117465.explanation: The VVD supports accessibility. The municipality can financially assist entrepreneurs, but the entrepreneur remains responsible for realizing and maintaining an accessible building.

117466.explanation: The VVD finds sports important, but municipal support should be targeted and responsible. Individuals remain responsible for their own sports memberships; guidance and attention to a healthy lifestyle are preferable to paying membership fees.

117471.explanation: The VVD considers monument care important, but preservation must be financially responsible. The municipality scrutinizes expenditures critically and prioritizes monuments with historical value and social relevance to use budgets efficiently.

117472.explanation: The VVD wants residents to feel safe. More enforcement officers contribute to supervision, tackling nuisance, and rule enforcement. We want to increase their number due to the effectiveness of administrative enforcement, such as tackling residential nuisance and vandalism.

117474.explanation: The VVD believes victims of domestic violence must have safe shelter. A domestic violence shelter is important and must be organized effectively, safely, and financially responsibly in cooperation with surrounding municipalities. Not every municipality needs to have one.

117475.explanation: When our enforcement officers address people’s behavior, they must have the necessary means. Especially now that societal tensions have hardened. Enforcement officers regularly face aggression and must be able to defend themselves.

117479.explanation: The VVD sees opportunities for transformation, but subsidies are not a standard solution. Owners must take responsibility for conversion themselves. The municipality can stimulate this through regulations, advice, or temporary support.

117480.explanation: The VVD believes social assistance should be focused on participation and self-reliance. Socially meaningful (volunteer) work helps people build skills, maintain structure, and contribute to the community alongside financial support.

117482.explanation: The VVD wants poverty to be addressed in a targeted manner. Raising income thresholds does not directly lead to the desired effect. Assistance must be focused on the most vulnerable. This group is not always defined by income alone, but also by high expenses, such as high healthcare costs.

117484.explanation: The VVD promotes sustainable mobility. This includes that the availability of charging points keeps pace with the increasing purchase of electric vehicles.

117487.explanation: The VVD values freedom of choice in healthcare. Everyone should be able to choose a healthcare provider that fits their religion, culture, or beliefs, as long as this is feasible, safe, and within available resources.

117489.explanation: Lowering the property tax (ozb) reduces the financial burden for residents and ensures affordable housing costs, provided it is possible within the municipal budget. If a reduction is not possible, we advocate for a property tax that remains the same or is only increased in line with trends.

117492.explanation: The VVD stimulates the local economy and employment. Expanding business parks can be attractive, provided it fits spatially. Within our municipality, we are working on expansion by developing the Stormpolderdijk.

117494.explanation: The VVD believes that local residents should have first right to affordable housing. Refugees receive appropriate housing but should not structurally have priority over local housing seekers.

117496.explanation: The VVD encourages sustainable energy, but farmland is primarily for food production. Solar panels may only be installed where this is feasible, responsible, and does not come at the expense of agriculture, for example on roofs.

117500.explanation: The VVD finds that coffeeshops should only be allowed if this can be done safely and responsibly. Their establishment depends on local circumstances, enforcement, and livability. The municipality must carefully weigh whether a shop contributes to or detracts from the neighborhood.

117502.explanation: The VVD wants housing construction that fits the character of Krimpen. High residential flats can be built in locations that do not damage the village character and livability, as is already the case in our municipality.

117503.explanation: The VVD believes the housing market should be flexible. Renting out individual rooms can help provide affordable housing for students or starters, provided it is safe, responsible, and includes clear agreements on livability and supervision. The VVD also supports splitting homes.

117504.explanation: In Krimpen, we have sufficient availability of neighborhood rooms where various activities are organized. The VVD considers meeting places important, but not every neighborhood has enough demand or resources for its own community center. The focus is on efficient use of existing facilities.

117505.explanation: The VVD wants the center to remain accessible and attractive for residents and entrepreneurs. Additional parking spaces, if there is demand, help traffic and the economy, provided they are implemented smartly, feasibly, and with regard for livability.

117506.explanation: The VVD wants an accessible and lively center. A fully car-free center limits accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs. Alternatives such as car-sharing, traffic calming measures, and smart routing are preferred.

117507.explanation: The VVD believes property owners bear responsibility for their real estate. The municipality can encourage, stimulate, and facilitate, for example with permits or advice, but must not enforce how vacancy is resolved. Coercion is not an option.

117509.explanation: The VVD promotes entrepreneurship and vitality. More events are welcome, provided they are organized safely, feasibly, and balanced with the living environment, so residents and entrepreneurs benefit. The municipality does not always have to fund these itself.

117451.explanation: Quality takes precedence over gender ratio.

117455.explanation: Complete freedom of store opening hours can be detrimental to small entrepreneurs. The CDA also values Sunday rest. Restaurants may open more flexibly but with attention to balancing economic interests and the livability for neighbors.

117458.explanation: The CDA supports fair and distributed shelter where all municipalities contribute their part. The CDA strives for a just and humane asylum policy that focuses on controlling inflow and maintaining societal support.

117463.explanation: The CDA favors allowing bulky waste to be collected free of charge at home several times a year. This is already the case for garden waste.

117465.explanation: With the introduction of an accessibility subsidy, barriers should literally and figuratively be lowered so that people with disabilities have good access to all locations.

117466.explanation: The CDA supports the 'Everyone Participates Fund' so that adults and children with low incomes can participate in sports, culture, and social activities.

117471.explanation: In Krimpen we have no municipal monuments, but we do have landmark objects with cultural-historical value. The CDA thinks it is important that the municipality maintains these and especially does not cut funding. Cultural heritage must be cherished and passed on to the next generation.

117472.explanation: By increasing focus on safety and expanding the number of municipal enforcement officers (boa's), you ensure timely detection of issues. Visible presence and approachability of boa's contribute to a safe living environment.

117474.explanation: There are strong regional cooperation agreements within the social domain. Specialized regional facilities are used. Residents dealing with domestic violence can turn to KrimpenWijzer.

117475.explanation: The authority to carry a baton belongs only to the police.

117479.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of converting vacant office buildings into homes. This should not come at the expense of employment. The CDA advocates a more active approach by the municipality against long-term vacancy to prevent urban decay.

117480.explanation: Existential security for the CDA is not only about income but also about purpose and being part of society. It is good to actively encourage people receiving social assistance to participate in the labor process, if possible. This should never be a 'trade-off'.

117482.explanation: For a few years now, our municipality has raised this threshold to 130%. The CDA has always supported this increase. We do not agree to raise the income threshold again. The already implemented increase is now acceptable.

117484.explanation: The CDA supports electric driving, but extra charging points in public spaces should not cause unnecessary parking pressure in residential neighborhoods.

117487.explanation: The CDA advocates that residents always receive good and proper care. If the situation allows, the resident should be able to choose which healthcare provider best suits his/her beliefs.

117489.explanation: The CDA finds it important to keep housing costs affordable. Increasing burdens should only be used as a last resort to achieve a balanced budget. The municipality must critically examine where it can cut costs before raising the property tax.

117492.explanation: However, in Krimpen aan den IJssel, there is actually no room to expand business parks. The municipal boundary has been reached. It may be possible to arrange business parks more efficiently.

117494.explanation: The pressure on the housing market has become too great. Therefore, the CDA believes that status holders can no longer have priority over regular housing seekers. Like at the national level, we advocate for more control over migration.

117496.explanation: The CDA believes agricultural land should not be sacrificed for placing solar panels. There are plenty of flat roofs suitable for this purpose.

117502.explanation: The CDA is not against high-rise buildings, but they must be suitable for the location and character of the neighborhood. The village character of Krimpen must not be lost, and green spaces must be preserved. A considered choice will always be made with attention to the location.

117503.explanation: Given the high pressure on the housing market, the CDA finds this should be possible. However, renting out a room must always be for residential use.

117504.explanation: The CDA values the presence of neighborhood rooms in the districts highly. They promote social connection within a neighborhood. Caring for each other is of great importance to the CDA.

117505.explanation: Krimpen aan den IJssel is a compact municipality where facilities are easily accessible by bike or on foot. The CDA finds it important to encourage cycling and walking. Additional parking spaces in the center are also unnecessary in our view.

117506.explanation: The CDA prefers to make the center largely car-free. However, entrepreneurs must remain easily accessible both for our residents and for distribution purposes. The safety of our residents is paramount. Emergency services must always be able to access the center well.

117507.explanation: According to the CDA, coercion does not work. We must engage in continuous dialogue so that a joint plan can be made to reduce vacancies. The municipality should actively support entrepreneurs in this.

117509.explanation: The CDA is a strong supporter of the Krimpen Meets fund. The municipality should play a facilitating and stimulating role in this. Initiatives from residents should be supported by the municipality.

117451.explanation: Our goal is to have an equal number of men and women in the council. However, these candidates must be available, and ultimately it should be about the quality of the candidates. Additionally, the council consists of 5 members, so an equal number is impossible.

117455.explanation: We must get rid of the early event closing times currently in place in Krimpen. For example, an event on a Saturday could be allowed to continue until 1:00 AM.

117458.explanation: In Krimpen aan den IJssel, refugees are accommodated with broad support and the efforts of many volunteers. The 'asylum boat' has not been a burden for years, and people from Ukraine are well accommodated in the new project on the Dijk. In the long term, these homes will become available for people from Krimpen.

117463.explanation: Especially for elderly and less mobile people, who cannot just go to the waste disposal station themselves.

117465.explanation: Accessibility must be a given! The municipality is responsible for locations under its own management. Buildings owned by entrepreneurs are the responsibility of those entrepreneurs. We encourage cooperation with interest groups for people with disabilities.

117466.explanation: A regulation already exists for this, and it should remain in place.

117471.explanation: Monuments are important for our cultural heritage. Where applicable in Krimpen, these monuments deserve our support (for example, the old cemetery by the dike).

117472.explanation: We currently already have BOA officers. We pay them as a municipality ourselves, unlike police officers who are paid by the national government. 'Our' officers are also often deployed outside Krimpen. Certainly, community officers should be present full-time in Krimpen. They can support the BOAs.

117474.explanation: Women (and men) must have a safe shelter in crisis situations. If such a project needs to be realized in Krimpen, it has our support. It is important that this location remains 'secret' to guarantee safety.

117475.explanation: The monopoly on the use of force must be reserved for and remain with the police, the military, and special counter-terrorism units. A BOA must act with social skills and be de-escalating. In emergencies, they must be able to immediately call the police using a panic button on their uniform.

117479.explanation: Wherever this opportunity arises, we must seize it. For example, the office building opposite the town hall is perfectly suitable for this. Temporary social rental housing for young people could be realized there.

117480.explanation: Returning to work may be possible through a social internship if it genuinely offers prospects for permanent and full paid employment. For people who truly cannot do this due to a (medical) disability, it must never become an obligation.

117482.explanation: Due to enormous inflation, making ends meet is almost or already impossible for many families. Wherever possible, we must help these people and families.

117484.explanation: More and more driving will be electric. It is therefore obviously necessary that these cars can be charged at sufficient locations.

117487.explanation: Where possible, people should be free to make their own choices. Only if their preference cannot be fulfilled should an alternative be sought.

117489.explanation: Residents of Krimpen are not a cash cow for the municipality. The municipality should first fully review its own organization to see if savings can be made, for example, by reducing the number of aldermen.

117492.explanation: There are already enough business parks in Krimpen (including the Stormpolder). Additionally, there is absolutely no space left for expansion.

117494.explanation: Regional agreements have already been made on this. Moreover, local residents already have priority for houses.

117496.explanation: We are not opposed to this, but we do not have agricultural land.

117500.explanation: We are not against coffee shops; however, Krimpen has too many primary schools in a small area. Due to distance restrictions, a coffee shop is not possible. Residents of Krimpen can go to Rotterdam for a coffee shop.

117502.explanation: We are and remain a small municipality with a lot of low-rise buildings; this is Krimpen's charm. We are fine with an apartment complex with multiple floors, as we already have those. We do not need 'tower blocks'; after all, we do not live in New York.

117503.explanation: This would greatly help students studying in Rotterdam, as there is a huge shortage of student rooms.

117504.explanation: There are already many neighborhood centers (community living rooms) in Krimpen, and they are important to our municipality. We will continue to support this approach.

117505.explanation: There are already more than enough parking spaces in the center—so many that the parking lot at the event grounds is usually empty.

117506.explanation: In the redevelopment of the square in front of the town hall, we want a car-free square so that beautiful and large terraces can be established there. The rest of the center remains accessible by car.

117507.explanation: Vacancy is unacceptable, and we do not want a half-empty shopping center. The municipality must come up with a concrete public plan for a new central area, for users of the Tuyter, apartments, modern stores, and horeca establishments with terraces.

117509.explanation: Wherever someone wants to organize an event, that should be possible. The 'festival week' like in Lekkerkerk could also be a nice event for the youth and families in Krimpen.

117451.explanation: Preferably an equal distribution, but quality comes first. So if there are more suitable women than men, that is also fine according to D66.

117455.explanation: It is desirable to consult with local residents about this.

117458.explanation: We are in favor of generous and appropriate shelter for all who need it.

117463.explanation: D66 supports free monthly waste collection.

117465.explanation: This is the entrepreneurs' own responsibility. Support and information from the municipality is a good idea.

117466.explanation: Sports and vitality are important. Various arrangements already exist. Harmonizing the existing support schemes is necessary, as there is currently overlap between programs.

117471.explanation: Heritage is important to preserve. Attention must be and remain given to our history.

117472.explanation: There are sufficient BOAs in Krimpen.

117474.explanation: Active policies to combat violence against women and girls, and help for victims, are of utmost importance.

117475.explanation: BOAs should not take over police powers.

117479.explanation: In our municipality as well, there is too much vacant office and retail space that could be better used to create housing.

117480.explanation: It is good to ask everyone to actively contribute to society and invite them to do so, but obliging people goes too far for us.

117482.explanation: Poverty is a growing problem. It is important to support people who need it so they can continue (or start) to fully participate in society.

117484.explanation: Sufficient charging points are being provided at the moment.

117487.explanation: For D66, it is important that everyone can make their own choice in this.

117489.explanation: Taxes should not be higher than necessary. But if by doing so we can achieve a better level of services, then we should be able to discuss that.

117492.explanation: The Stormpolderdijk should finally become available for development.

117494.explanation: To address the reception crisis, all municipalities must contribute proportionally to help refugees with residence permits find housing.

117496.explanation: Incidentally, there is hardly any farmland in Krimpen.

117500.explanation: A coffeeshop is a better solution than the uncontrolled sales as they take place now.

117502.explanation: If we want to realize more homes, this is inevitable because there is much too little space within the municipality. But everything in moderation.

117503.explanation: This could contribute to increasing the supply of housing.

117504.explanation: That is already the case in Krimpen.

117505.explanation: There is sufficient parking space. Krimpen is a small municipality so you can reach everything in the center well by bike or public transport.

117506.explanation: Car-free is going too far, but car-reduced would be a good step.

117507.explanation: Coercion will not work, but actively working on a solution together with the owners would be very good.

117509.explanation: Krimpen can definitely become a bit more nice. That isn't only up to the municipality. It also requires more private initiative.

statements.title: The municipality should force the owners of the De Crimpenhof shopping center to resolve the vacancy problem.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de eigenaren van winkelcentrum De Crimpenhof dwingen om de

titleParts.glossary: When buildings or homes are not used and are therefore vacant.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Krimpen aan den IJssel should employ more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.text: In Krimpen aan den IJssel moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Krimpen aan den IJssel should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Krimpen aan den IJssel controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Krimpen aan den IJssel should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.text: In Krimpen aan den IJssel moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: High residential towers should be permitted in Krimpen aan den IJssel.
The center of Krimpen aan den IJssel should become car-free.
Business parks in Krimpen aan den IJssel should be allowed to expand.
Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The municipality should allow more events.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.
